## Transaction Confirmation — Frontend System Design

This is a common **Frontend System Design / React interview** question.

Examples:

* UPI Payment Confirmation
* Credit Card Payment Success
* Bank Transfer Confirmation
* E-commerce Order Confirmation
* Stock Buy/Sell Confirmation

***

# Requirements Gathering

## Functional Requirements

### Before Transaction

```text
Review Transaction
↓
Confirm
↓
API Call
```

### During Transaction

```text
Show Loader
Disable Button
Prevent Duplicate Submission
```

### After Success

```text
Txn ID
Amount
Recipient
Date/Time
Status
```

### After Failure

```text
Error Message
Retry Button
Contact Support
```

***

# High Level Architecture

```text
Confirmation Page
       │
       ▼
Transaction Service
       │
       ▼
Payment API
       │
       ▼
Status Polling / WebSocket
       │
       ▼
Success / Failure Screen
```

***

# Component Design

```tsx
<TransactionConfirmation />
```

Sub Components:

```text
├── TransactionSummary
├── ConfirmButton
├── Loader
├── SuccessState
├── FailureState
├── TransactionReceipt
```

***

# Data Model

```ts
interface Transaction {
  transactionId: string;

  amount: number;

  currency: string;

  recipient: string;

  status:
    | "PENDING"
    | "SUCCESS"
    | "FAILED";

  createdAt: string;
}
```

***

# API Design

## Initiate Transaction

```http
POST /transactions
```

Request

```json
{
  "amount": 5000,
  "recipientId": "user123"
}
```

Response

```json
{
  "transactionId": "TXN001",
  "status": "PENDING"
}
```

***

## Check Transaction Status

```http
GET /transactions/TXN001
```

Response

```json
{
  "transactionId": "TXN001",
  "status": "SUCCESS"
}
```

***

# React Implementation

## State

```tsx
const [status, setStatus] =
  useState<
    "IDLE" |
    "PENDING" |
    "SUCCESS" |
    "FAILED"
  >("IDLE");

const [transactionId,
       setTransactionId] =
  useState("");
```

***

## Confirm Transaction

```tsx
const handleConfirm =
  async () => {
    try {
      setStatus("PENDING");

      const response =
        await createTransaction();

      setTransactionId(
        response.transactionId
      );

      pollStatus(
        response.transactionId
      );
    } catch {
      setStatus("FAILED");
    }
  };
```

***

# Status Polling

Many payment gateways are asynchronous.

```tsx
async function pollStatus(id) {
  const interval =
    setInterval(async () => {
      const result =
        await fetchStatus(id);

      if (
        result.status ===
        "SUCCESS"
      ) {
        setStatus("SUCCESS");
        clearInterval(interval);
      }

      if (
        result.status ===
        "FAILED"
      ) {
        setStatus("FAILED");
        clearInterval(interval);
      }
    }, 2000);
}
```

***

# Prevent Double Payment

Very important interview point.

### Disable Button

```tsx
<button
 disabled={
   status === "PENDING"
 }
>
  Confirm
</button>
```

***

### Idempotency Key

Frontend:

```ts
const idempotencyKey =
 crypto.randomUUID();
```

Request:

```http
POST /transactions
Idempotency-Key: abc123
```

If user clicks twice:

```text
Backend returns same transaction
```

No duplicate payments.

***

# UI States

## Initial

```text
Amount ₹5000
Recipient John

[Confirm]
```

***

## Loading

```text
Processing Payment...

⏳
```

***

## Success

```text
✅ Payment Successful

Txn Id:
TXN001

Amount:
₹5000

Date:
02-Jul-2026
```

***

## Failed

```text
❌ Transaction Failed

Reason:
Insufficient Balance

[Retry]
```

***

# Receipt Component

```tsx
<TransactionReceipt
 transactionId={id}
 amount={5000}
 recipient="John"
/>
```

Display:

```text
Transaction Id
Amount
Timestamp
Status
```

***

# Real-Time Updates

Instead of polling:

## WebSocket

```text
Frontend
   │
WebSocket
   │
Backend
```

Backend pushes:

```json
{
  "transactionId": "TXN001",
  "status": "SUCCESS"
}
```

Benefits:

* Faster updates
* Less network traffic

***

# Error Handling

### Network Errors

```tsx
try {
   await apiCall();
}
catch(error){
   showToast();
}
```

***

### Retry Strategy

```tsx
Retry 3 times

1s
2s
4s
```

Exponential backoff.

***

# Security Considerations

### Never Trust Frontend Amount

Wrong:

```json
{
  "amount": 5000
}
```

Frontend value can be modified.

Backend should validate:

```text
Order
Amount
User
Balance
```

***

### Sensitive Data

Never expose:

```text
Card Number
CVV
PIN
```

Store only:

```text
Txn Id
Masked Number
```

***

# Accessibility

&lt;div role="alert" aria-live="polite"&gt; Payment Successful&lt;/div&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Screen readers announce status changes automatically.

***

# Performance Optimizations

### Lazy Load Receipt

```tsx
const Receipt = React.lazy(
 () => import("./Receipt")
);
```

### Cache Transaction Status

```tsx
React Query
```

```tsx
useQuery({
 queryKey: [
   "transaction",
   id,
 ],
 queryFn:
   fetchTransactionStatus,
});
```

***

# Senior-Level Discussion Points

Explain:

✅ Optimistic vs pessimistic updates

✅ Idempotency keys

✅ Polling vs WebSocket

✅ Retry with exponential backoff

✅ Transaction state machine

✅ Analytics tracking

✅ Fraud prevention

✅ Duplicate submission prevention

***

# State Machine

```text
IDLE
 ↓
PENDING
 ├── SUCCESS
 └── FAILED
```

This state-machine approach is typically what interviewers expect from a **Senior React Developer / Frontend Lead** when designing a transaction confirmation flow for banking, payments, or e-commerce applications.


Below is a **complete React + TypeScript Transaction Confirmation Flow** suitable for a **Senior React / Frontend System Design interview**.

Features:

✅ Transaction Summary  
✅ Confirm Transaction  
✅ Loading State  
✅ Success State  
✅ Failure State  
✅ Status Polling  
✅ Retry Button  
✅ Prevent Double Submission  
✅ Idempotency Key  
✅ Receipt Component  
✅ TypeScript

***

# Folder Structure

```text
src/
├── App.tsx
├── api/
│   └── transactionApi.ts
├── components/
│   ├── TransactionConfirmation.tsx
│   ├── TransactionSummary.tsx
│   ├── TransactionReceipt.tsx
│   └── StatusMessage.tsx
├── types/
│   └── transaction.ts
└── styles.css
```

***

# types/transaction.ts

```ts
export type TransactionStatus =
  | "IDLE"
  | "PENDING"
  | "SUCCESS"
  | "FAILED";

export interface Transaction {
  transactionId: string;
  amount: number;
  recipient: string;
  createdAt: string;
  status: TransactionStatus;
}
```

***

# api/transactionApi.ts

Mock API

```ts
export async function createTransaction() {
  await new Promise((resolve) =>
    setTimeout(resolve, 2000)
  );

  return {
    transactionId:
      "TXN-" + Date.now(),
    status: "PENDING",
  };
}

export async function getTransactionStatus(
  transactionId: string
) {
  await new Promise((resolve) =>
    setTimeout(resolve, 1000)
  );

  const success =
    Math.random() > 0.3;

  return {
    transactionId,
    status: success
      ? "SUCCESS"
      : "FAILED",
  };
}
```

***

# components/TransactionSummary.tsx

```tsx
interface Props {
  amount: number;
  recipient: string;
}

export default function TransactionSummary({
  amount,
  recipient,
}: Props) {
  return (
    <div className="card">
      <h3>Transaction Summary</h3>

      <p>
        <strong>Amount:</strong>
        ₹{amount}
      </p>

      <p>
        <strong>Recipient:</strong>
        {recipient}
      </p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# components/TransactionReceipt.tsx

```tsx
interface Props {
  transactionId: string;
  amount: number;
  recipient: string;
}

export default function TransactionReceipt({
  transactionId,
  amount,
  recipient,
}: Props) {
  return (
    <div className="receipt">
      <h3>
        Transaction Receipt
      </h3>

      <p>
        <strong>Transaction Id:</strong>
        {transactionId}
      </p>

      <p>
        <strong>Amount:</strong>
        ₹{amount}
      </p>

      <p>
        <strong>Recipient:</strong>
        {recipient}
      </p>

      <p>
        <strong>Date:</strong>
        {new Date().toLocaleString()}
      </p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# components/StatusMessage.tsx

```tsx
interface Props {
  type:
    | "SUCCESS"
    | "FAILED"
    | "PENDING";
}

export default function StatusMessage({
  type,
}: Props) {
  if (type === "PENDING") {
    return (
      <div className="status pending">
        Processing Transaction...
      </div>
    );
  }

  if (type === "SUCCESS") {
    return (
      <div className="status success">
        ✅ Transaction Successful
      </div>
    );
  }

  return (
    <div className="status failed">
      ❌ Transaction Failed
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# components/TransactionConfirmation.tsx

```tsx
import {
  useRef,
  useState,
} from "react";

import {
  createTransaction,
  getTransactionStatus,
} from "../api/transactionApi";

import StatusMessage from "./StatusMessage";
import TransactionReceipt from "./TransactionReceipt";
import TransactionSummary from "./TransactionSummary";

type Status =
  | "IDLE"
  | "PENDING"
  | "SUCCESS"
  | "FAILED";

export default function TransactionConfirmation() {
  const [status, setStatus] =
    useState<Status>("IDLE");

  const [
    transactionId,
    setTransactionId,
  ] = useState("");

  const [
    loading,
    setLoading,
  ] = useState(false);

  const amount = 5000;
  const recipient =
    "John Smith";

  const idempotencyRef =
    useRef(
      crypto.randomUUID()
    );

  const pollStatus =
    async (id: string) => {
      let attempts = 0;

      const interval =
        setInterval(
          async () => {
            attempts++;

            const result =
              await getTransactionStatus(
                id
              );

            if (
              result.status ===
              "SUCCESS"
            ) {
              setStatus(
                "SUCCESS"
              );

              clearInterval(
                interval
              );

              return;
            }

            if (
              result.status ===
              "FAILED"
            ) {
              setStatus(
                "FAILED"
              );

              clearInterval(
                interval
              );

              return;
            }

            if (
              attempts >= 5
            ) {
              setStatus(
                "FAILED"
              );
              clearInterval(
                interval
              );
            }
          },
          2000
        );
    };

  const handleConfirm =
    async () => {
      if (loading) return;

      try {
        setLoading(true);
        setStatus("PENDING");

        console.log(
          "Idempotency Key:",
          idempotencyRef.current
        );

        const response =
          await createTransaction();

        setTransactionId(
          response.transactionId
        );

        pollStatus(
          response.transactionId
        );
      } catch {
        setStatus("FAILED");
      } finally {
        setLoading(false);
      }
    };

  const retry =
    () => {
      setStatus("IDLE");
      setTransactionId("");
    };

  return (
    <div className="container">
      <TransactionSummary
        amount={amount}
        recipient={recipient}
      />

      {status === "IDLE" && (
        <button
          onClick={
            handleConfirm
          }
          disabled={
            loading
          }
        >
          Confirm
          Transaction
        </button>
      )}

      {status ===
        "PENDING" && (
        <StatusMessage
          type="PENDING"
        />
      )}

      {status ===
        "SUCCESS" && (
        <>
          <StatusMessage
            type="SUCCESS"
          />

          <TransactionReceipt
            transactionId={
              transactionId
            }
            amount={amount}
            recipient={
              recipient
            }
          />
        </>
      )}

      {status ===
        "FAILED" && (
        <>
          <StatusMessage
            type="FAILED"
          />

          <button
            onClick={
              retry
            }
          >
            Retry
          </button>
        </>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# App.tsx

```tsx
import TransactionConfirmation from "./components/TransactionConfirmation";
import "./styles.css";

export default function App() {
  return (
    <div>
      <h1>
        Transaction
        Confirmation
      </h1>

      <TransactionConfirmation />
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# styles.css

```css
body {
  font-family: Arial,
    sans-serif;

  background: #f4f4f4;

  padding: 40px;
}

.container {
  width: 500px;

  margin: auto;

  background: white;

  padding: 24px;

  border-radius: 10px;

  box-shadow: 0 4px
    15px
    rgba(
      0,
      0,
      0,
      0.1
    );
}

.card {
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

button {
  padding: 12px
    20px;

  border: none;

  background: #2563eb;

  color: white;

  border-radius: 6px;

  cursor: pointer;
}

button:disabled {
  opacity: 0.6;
}

.status {
  margin: 20px 0;

  padding: 12px;

  border-radius: 6px;
}

.success {
  background: #d1fae5;
  color: #065f46;
}

.failed {
  background: #fee2e2;
  color: #991b1b;
}

.pending {
  background: #dbeafe;
  color: #1e40af;
}

.receipt {
  margin-top: 20px;

  padding: 16px;

  border: 1px solid #ddd;
}
```

***

## Senior-Level Enhancements (Interview Discussion)

```text
✅ React Query/TanStack Query

✅ WebSocket instead of Polling

✅ Exponential Retry

✅ Optimistic Updates

✅ Redux/Zustand Store

✅ Analytics Tracking

✅ Fraud Prevention

✅ Duplicate Transaction Protection

✅ Receipt Download PDF

✅ Transaction Timeline

✅ State Machine (XState)
```

### State Machine

```text
IDLE
 ↓
PENDING
 ├── SUCCESS
 └── FAILED
```

This is the sort of complete implementation and architecture discussion expected for a **Senior React / Frontend Lead transaction confirmation system design round**.
